home.hero_desc:
Submit a request to create an introduction map
for new or updated products.

request.title: New Request
request.subtitle: Product Introduction Map Request Form
request.submit_confirm: Are you sure you want to submit this request? It cannot be edited after submission.
request.submit_note_placeholder: Enter any special notes to include with your submission. (Optional)
request.peer_review_banner: Under Review — Submitting after edit will proceed to the R stage.
request.submit_success: Your request has been successfully submitted!
request.messenger_sent_to_manager: An email notification has been sent to the reviewer.
request.auto_save_success: Auto-saved as draft after 20 minutes of inactivity.
request.map_type_change_confirm_msg: Changing the request type will reset all entered values. Do you want to continue?
request.only_map_confirm_msg: Selecting Only MAP will reset and disable Other Purpose, Flow Chart, Notes, Backbone, and Reference Request. Do you want to continue?
request.map_x_sign_error: North/South X values must have the same absolute value with opposite signs.
request.map_y_equal_error: North/South Y values must be identical.
request.toast_merge_complete: J-ayer {{jayerMatched}} pre-registered / O-ayer {{oayerMatched}} pre-registered, {{unmatched}} unmatched added
request.jayer_no_layer_data: No Layer data found in J-ayer information.
request.ext_data_assign_to_jayer: ② Select external data — assigned to the selected J-ayer row
request.tbvtlv_no_data: No TBV/TLV items found in OVL Layer list.
request.tbvtlv_warn_body: Thickness / Note fields are not filled in. Are you sure you want to proceed without entering them?
request.bb_overwrite_confirm: Existing Backbone data found. Do you want to overwrite it?
request.bb_overwrite_confirm_with_layers:
The following Layers already have data: {{layers}}
Do you want to overwrite them?
request.bb_partial_add_confirm: Do you want to add {{count}} non-duplicate row(s)?
request.bb_reset_confirm: Are you sure you want to clear all Backbone data?
request.special_care_confirm: Special Care (including Shot map) requests must be filled in. Do you want to proceed anyway?
request.filter_delete_confirm: Are you sure you want to delete the "{{label}}" filter?
request.filter_all_delete_confirm: Are you sure you want to delete all saved filters?

approval.title: Approval Status
approval.subtitle: Check the approval progress of submitted requests
approval.search_placeholder: Search by title, product, or requester
approval.withdraw_confirm: Are you sure you want to withdraw this request? It will return to draft status.
approval.resubmit_confirm: Are you sure you want to resubmit after editing?

route_diagram.title: Final Approval Route
route_diagram.desc: The approval stages a request goes through from intake to completion.
route_diagram.note_e: The EUV (E) stage runs only when plel (yellow cell) items are present.
route_diagram.note_onlymap: 'Only MAP' requests proceed only up to RFG (R); later stages are skipped.
route_diagram.note_reject: If rejected at any stage, the whole request becomes 'Rejected' and no further stages proceed; the requester can resubmit it as a new round from the start (PL review) by designating a peer PL, or withdraw it back to a draft.

history.title: History
history.subtitle: View all request creation and processing history
history.search_placeholder: Search by title, product, or requester

voc.title: VOC
voc.subtitle: Voice of Customer - Submit and manage customer feedback
voc.delete_confirm_body: Are you sure you want to delete this VOC? All comments and history will be permanently deleted.

login.title:
Product Introduction Map
Request System
login.subtitle: Sign in with your company account to continue.
login.hint: Authentication is handled via corporate OpenID.

permission.title: Permission Management
permission.subtitle: Add or remove users by role.
permission.delete_modal_body: Are you sure you want to delete the permission granted to this user?
permission.select_user_placeholder: Search by name, login ID, email, or department...
permission.select_user_error: Please select at least one user.
permission.search_placeholder: Search by name, login ID, email, or department...

notice.title: Title
notice.delete_confirm: Are you sure you want to delete "{{title}}"?

session.warning_body:
You have been inactive for a while.
Would you like to extend your session?

route.title: Approval Route
route.description: The final approval route of this system. After Review (PL) and RFG, Route 1 (PHPSI→JOB) and Route 2 (OVL·EUV) proceed in parallel. (E only when plel exists; MAP-only requests stop at R.)

request.title: Create a Request
request.description: Select a type, enter a title and details, then submit to file your request.

flow.wizard: A request is completed through a 5-step wizard, in order.
flow.detail: Once you fill in line, recipe, product and process, the disabled sections below become editable.
flow.required: Fields marked with a red * are required — fill them in before moving on.
flow.map: On the MAP step, press the guide (?) button for anything you're unsure about.
flow.jayer_auto: J-ayer is auto-filled from your request details, and you can edit it with cell drag and copy/paste.
flow.jayer_filter: Use the filter to pick and disable only the rows you want.
flow.oayer: O-ayer has two tabs — be sure to fill in both.
flow.bb_autofill: On the BB step, 'Auto Fill' populates Backbone data in one click.
flow.bb_mapping: You can also map BB data directly by clicking a source row.
flow.submit_note: When submitting, add notes to pass along to the reviewer.
flow.submit_designee: You must assign a reviewer (PL) before submitting.
flow.submitted: On submit, a 'Request submitted' notification appears and approval begins.
flow.submit_note_sample: Requesting review for the new line introduction.
flow.map_purpose: First choose the correct MAP purpose (new / clone / registered).
flow.map_cfamily: Fill in each MAP field to match the product. For example, select 'Yes' for a C-family product to enter the additional fields.
flow.map_deviation: If the map has a deviation, switch to 'Has change' and enter the X/Y correction values and reason.
flow.map_exception: If there is an exception zone, switch to 'Has change' and enter the exception zone value.
flow.map_xmark_photo: If the X-mark changes, pick the option and paste an actual photo into the change area.
flow.oayer_info_tbvtlv: The 'Info' tab holds Partial Shot and TBV/TLV. TBV/TLV only appears when the O-ayer table contains TBV/TLV items.
flow.submit_combined: On submit you can add notes and must designate a reviewer (PL) before submitting.
flow.bb_autofill_apply: Click 'Apply' with the cursor to run auto-fill and generate the BB rows at once.

approval.title: Approval Status
approval.description: Check the approval route and progress of submitted requests, and export tables to Excel from the detail view.

flow.intro: The Approval Status page lets you track the progress of submitted requests stage by stage.
flow.route: This is the final approval route a request goes through. After Review (PL) and RFG, Path 1 (PHPSI→JOB) and Path 2 (OVL·EUV) run in parallel.
flow.my_filter: The 'MY' filter shows only requests you submitted or that need your approval.
flow.stage_mail: The 'Current Stage' column shows the active approval step and its assignee, and a mail is sent automatically to the relevant team/assignee.
flow.assign_btn: For a step with no assignee yet, click 'Assign' in the detail, pick an approver from the same-role (team) members, and confirm.
flow.assign_result: On 'Confirm', the approver is assigned to that step and a mail is sent to the designated assignee.
flow.open_detail: Click the title with the cursor to open the request detail.
flow.detail_tabs: Click the tabs at the top of the detail (Detail·MAP·JOB·OVL·BB·Approval Route) to review each part of the request you submitted.
flow.export_jayer: Use the 📊 export button on the JOB tab to download the table data as an Excel file.
flow.route_tab: The Approval Route tab shows each team’s (Review/RFG/PHPSI/JOB/OVL/EUV) approve/reject history with assignees and dates, by round.
flow.revision_diff: A request resubmitted after rejection highlights the changed fields/rows, and a 'View History' button appears on changed rows.
flow.revision_modal: Click 'View History' to compare the before/after values side by side.

permission.title: Permissions
permission.description: Add users to a role tab, then group same-role users together. A group is visible only to its members, and notification mails go only to members.

guide.title: Guide Management
guide.subtitle: Manage guides for request form
guide.type_feature_desc: Linked to a specific form feature · Guide button auto-created on save
guide.type_info_desc: Standalone informational document · Viewable in guide list
guide.paste_image_hint: You can paste images directly into the editor with Ctrl+V.
guide.confirm_delete: Are you sure you want to delete this guide?
guide.feature_already_exists: A guide for this feature already exists.
guide.author: Author
guide.panel_hint: You can view this guide while filling out the form
guide.panel_form_hint: Click outside the panel to interact with the form

step1_line_process.lead: This is the first required item when creating a request. Select the 4 fields below in order to enable the remaining items.
step1_line_process.downstream_title: Request Purpose · Flow Chart · BB Combination …
step1_line_process.callout: ⚠️ Note · The process/part/recipe lists are pulled from an external query DB and refreshed once every hour. If a newly registered item is missing, try again shortly.

step3_jayer_table.lead: The core J-ayer table. Copy a product name and drag-paste it, and STEP auto-fills by Layer while the ID (barcode) auto-matches. Row disable/restore is also supported.
step3_jayer_table.phase_copy: ① Copy a single product-name cell (Ctrl+C)
step3_jayer_table.phase_paste: ② Drag-select multiple cells and paste the product name (Ctrl+V)
step3_jayer_table.phase_dropdown: ⑤ With 2 candidates, check the dropdown and pick one
step3_jayer_table.phase_disable: ⑥ Check rows, then disable the selection
step3_jayer_table.phase_restore: ⑦ Restore to revert to the original state
step3_jayer_table.callout: ⚠️ Note · STEP/barcode candidates come from an external query DB refreshed hourly. A single barcode match auto-fills; with 2+ matches, pick from the dropdown. Disabled rows are excluded from the request.
step3_jayer_table.phase_step: ③ STEP auto-fills based on each row’s Layer

step3_jayer_filter.lead: The J-ayer table has a 'Filter' feature that disables matching rows at once. Build conditions with SP/SD/PP keywords, apply them, and edit or delete saved filters.
step3_jayer_filter.phase_create: ① + Filter → enter a name and keywords, then add
step3_jayer_filter.phase_apply: ② Click the filter button in the toolbar → matching rows are disabled
step3_jayer_filter.phase_edit: ③ Change keywords via [Edit] on a saved filter
step3_jayer_filter.phase_delete: ④ Remove the filter with [Delete] (clears the disabling too)
step3_jayer_filter.callout: 💡 Filters rows whose SP(STEPSEQ)/SD(STEP Desc)/PP(PPID) column contains the keyword. You can add multiple keywords; manage saved filters with [Edit]·[Delete].

step4_oayer_table.lead: O-ayer requires filling both tabs (OVL Layer List · OVL Info). The list tab works like the J-ayer table, and the info tab holds Partial Shot and TBV/TLV settings.
step4_oayer_table.phase_table: ① OVL Layer List tab — copy/paste product name → STEP auto-fills
step4_oayer_table.phase_switch: ② Switch to the OVL Info tab (fill both tabs)
step4_oayer_table.phase_partial: ③ Partial Shot required selection (O / X)
step4_oayer_table.phase_tbvtlv_cond: ④ TBV/TLV shows only when a row's SD contains TBV/TLV
step4_oayer_table.phase_tbvtlv_fill: ⑤ Enter thickness · select SD · note, then add
step4_oayer_table.table_note: Same as the J-ayer table (paste product name → STEP auto · barcode · disable/restore)
step4_oayer_table.tbvtlv_cond: Shown when a row's SD contains TBV/TLV
step4_oayer_table.callout: 💡 Both tabs must be filled. Partial Shot is required, and the TBV/TLV setting appears in the OVL Info tab only when an O-ayer row's SD contains TBV/TLV.

step5_bb_autofill.lead: Auto-fills BB data into J-ayer rows by Layer range. You can define multiple ranges at once.
step5_bb_autofill.phase_open: ① 📋 Backbone Auto-fill → open the Layer range panel
step5_bb_autofill.phase_range1: ② Range 1 — select start/end Layer and Product ID
step5_bb_autofill.phase_add: ③ Add another range with + Add Range
step5_bb_autofill.phase_range2: ④ Range 2 — select start/end Layer and Product ID
step5_bb_autofill.phase_apply: ⑤ ✔ Apply → BB data auto-fills the rows in range
step5_bb_autofill.callout: ⚠️ BB data is pulled from an external query DB and refreshed once every hour. Use + Add Range to add as many ranges as needed; on apply, the J-ayer rows in each range are filled automatically.

step5_bb_mapping.lead: Map BB data to each J-ayer row directly. Pick a source row on the left, then click a BB data row on the right to link them.
step5_bb_mapping.phase_select: ① Select a source row to map on the left
step5_bb_mapping.phase_map: ② Click a BB data row on the right → map
step5_bb_mapping.phase_apply: ④ ✔ Apply → mapped rows move to the applied result
step5_bb_mapping.apply_hint: Select a source row on the left and click BB data on the right to map.
step5_bb_mapping.callout: ⚠️ The BB data list on the right is pulled from an external query DB and refreshed once every hour. Mapped rows disappear from the left list and are added to the result; press the X button on a row to undo a mapping.

step2_map_type.lead: Select the MAP type (NEW·CLONE·EXISTING) and fill in details like Exception Zone, X mark, and MAP Options. Changing the type resets entered values.
step2_map_type.phase_new: ① Select NEW — enter each item directly
step2_map_type.phase_ea: ② Exception Zone → Has Change + value (mm)
step2_map_type.phase_xmark: ④ X mark → on Add, paste an image (Ctrl+V) into the area
step2_map_type.phase_options: ⑤ Toggle apply/none with MAP Option buttons (multi-select)
step2_map_type.phase_clone: ⑥ Switch to CLONE → values reset + pick Source Location·Product
step2_map_type.phase_existing: ⑦ EXISTING → reuse an existing MAP (most inputs disabled, just confirm)
step2_map_type.existing_note: Reuses an existing MAP as-is. Detailed inputs are disabled — just select.
step2_map_type.callout: 💡 NEW enters from scratch, CLONE copies a source product (pick Source Location·Product), EXISTING reuses an existing MAP. Changing the type resets entered values.
step2_map_type.phase_deviation: ③ MAP Deviation → Has Change + enter X·Y values

step2_cfamily.lead: The input flow when Only C-family = Yes. Product region, North/Middle/South areas, MAP deviation, X mark, and REV management are enabled.
step2_cfamily.phase_cfamily: ① Select Only C-family = Yes → enable dedicated items
step2_cfamily.phase_region: ② Select the product region → that area auto-fills with the current product (enter South manually)
step2_cfamily.phase_deviation: ③ MAP deviation — fixed to ‘Has Change’; enter North/South X·Y (X opposite signs, Y equal)
step2_cfamily.phase_xmark: ④ X mark — C-family pastes North/South images separately
step2_cfamily.phase_rev: ⑤ REV management — add GDS version per Layer
step2_cfamily.dev_rule: Rule: |X| equal · opposite signs / Y equal
step2_cfamily.rev_hint: Layer buttons = Layer IDs of active J-ayer rows
step2_cfamily.callout: 💡 Selecting Only C-family = Yes enables North/Middle/South areas (North·South required, Middle optional), MAP deviation (opposite N/S signs), North/South X mark images, and REV management. REV Layer buttons come from active J-ayer Layer IDs; enter a GDS version to add.

permission_user_group.lead: On the Permissions page, this is the flow for adding users to a role and creating user groups.
permission_user_group.phase_open_user: ① + Add User → open modal (title shows current role)
permission_user_group.phase_search_user: ② Search → each candidate shows its current role
permission_user_group.phase_add_user: ④ Add → assign all to the current role list
permission_user_group.phase_create_group: ⑧ Create → a group tab is added to the tab bar
permission_user_group.callout: 💡 Add User assigns users to the currently selected role tab. Create Group bundles users of the same role so they can be managed together.
permission_user_group.phase_group_intro: ⑤ About Create Group (emails go only to members)
permission_user_group.group_intro_desc: Bundle users of the same role for unified management. Once created, it is added as a group tab above; this group tab is visible only to its members, and notification emails are sent only to group members.
permission_user_group.intro_b1: Bundle users of the same role to manage them at once.
permission_user_group.intro_b2: The group tab is visible only to its members.
permission_user_group.intro_b3: Notification emails are sent only to group members.
permission_user_group.intro_b4: An author's draft requests are shared only with members of the same group.
permission_user_group.intro_b5: On creation, the creator (you) is automatically included as a member.
permission_user_group.cmp_note: Only members see the group tab, notification mail, and drafts

group.section_subtitle: Create and manage groups with users of the same role.
group.no_groups: You are not in any group.
group.add_member_placeholder: Search by name, login ID, email, or department...
group.remove_member_confirm_body: Are you sure you want to remove this member from the group?
group.leave_confirm_body: Are you sure you want to leave this group?
group.delete_confirm_body: Are you sure you want to delete "{{name}}"? This action cannot be undone.
group.no_role_error: Users without a role cannot create groups.
